title: lithocarpus chittagongus
intro: Lithocarpus chittagongus, also known as Chittagong oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to Bangladesh and India, belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Lithocarpus chittagongus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
